the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field has tried and lost with (-1.4% winrate over 24 games) — treat this opener as a sim blind-spot and weight the field

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 23. Take Demiurge: +15.6% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 1,369 damage in one combo in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 23 — about 4,526 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 4% of our best core on physical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 23 — about 207 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their burst combo hits 10% less hard, and this kit gives up 31% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their burst combo hits 12% less hard, and this kit gives up 29% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their burst combo hits 12% less hard, and this kit gives up 31% of its damage for it.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their burst combo hits 12% less hard, and this kit gives up 30% of its damage for it.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 811 damage in one combo in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 28 — about 154 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Catalytic Drive: their burst combo hits 20% less hard, and this kit gives up 0% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
